source_detail: Average Effective Tariff Rate — Policy through April 15, 2026 (Pre-Substitution)
title: Average Effective Tariff Rate
subtitle: Policy through April 15, 2026, Pre-Substitution
methodology: Each data point is the headline overall pre-substitution average effective US tariff rate produced by the Yale Budget Lab Tariff Rate Tracker. Methodology: Yale applies announced tariff schedules (MFN + Section 122 + Section 232 + Section 301 + any IEEPA authority in effect) at the HTS10 × trading-partner level to ~240 partners and ~20,000 HS10 product codes, weighted by pre-tariff import shares. Stacking rules: MFN is additive in all branches, Section 232 took precedence over IEEPA while IEEPA was in effect, Section 301 is always cumulative. Pre-substitution means the rate applies announced schedules to the pre-tariff import mix without adjusting for consumer/supplier behavioral substitution. Source pipeline: Budget-Lab-Yale/Tariff-ETRs on GitHub.

points.source: Yale Budget Lab methodology applied to current announced schedule

notes: Each point is the headline overall pre-substitution 'average effective US tariff rate' reported by the Yale Budget Lab in a 'State of U.S. Tariffs' update for the given date. The final April 15, 2026 point is a carry-forward of Yale's April 8 snapshot since no new tariff actions have been announced in the intervening week.

Yale methodology: announced tariff schedules (MFN, Section 122, Section 232, Section 301, and IEEPA while it was in effect) are applied at the HTS10 × trading-partner level to ~240 partners and ~20,000 HS10 product codes, weighted by pre-tariff import shares. Pre-substitution means no adjustment for consumer/supplier behavioral shifting away from higher-tariff goods. The live pipeline is open-source at github.com/Budget-Lab-Yale/Tariff-ETRs.

Key 2025 events: April 2 'Liberation Day' 10% universal baseline, April 9 revised reciprocal rates (peak ~28%), May 12 US-China 90-day pause, May 28 Court of International Trade ruling briefly vacating IEEPA, Federal Circuit stay restoring IEEPA tariffs, August 1 reciprocal tariffs effective, November 2025 agricultural exemptions.

Key 2026 events: January 19 Greenland tariffs added (~17.5% peak early 2026), February 20 Supreme Court vacates IEEPA tariffs (rate drops to 9.1% overnight), February 24 Section 122 10% temporary import surcharge takes effect (150-day authority, expires late July 2026), April 2 Section 232 proclamation restructuring metals tariffs (50% primary, 25% derivative, on full customs value) and announcing 100% Section 232 tariff on patented pharmaceuticals (phased in over 120-180 days).

Current tariff rate as of April 15, 2026: 11.8% pre-substitution, per Yale's April 8 snapshot carried forward. If Section 122 expires as scheduled in late July and the Section 232 pharma 100% tariff phases in on schedule, Yale projects the rate will settle at ~9.7% by late 2026; if Section 122 is made permanent, the end-of-2026 rate is projected at ~12.2%.
